chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.551 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs 1. For orders on M.A. No.3288/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.3289/2025 (Exp/A) 3. For hearing of bail application ------------------------- 03.03.2025 Mr. Nasrullah Malik, advocate for applicant ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Aqib Ali Shah son of Gul Zahir Shah seeks protective bail in FIR No.8/2020, registered at P.S. Anti-Encroachment Force, Zone-I, Karachi for offence under Section 8 of the Sindh Public Property (Removal of Encroachment) Act, 2010. Learned counsel for applicant submits that this Court vid order dated 20.04.2021, admitted the applicant to protective bail, therefore, he surrendered before the concerned trial Court and trial Court admitted him to interim pre-arrest bail and he faced trial but due to psychological issues he remained absent from trial Court. He further submits that after treatment, applicant is recovered and once again he intends to surrender before learned trial Court concerned and requests for suspension of NBWs issued against him by trial Court, in order to enable him to surrender before the trial Court. Be that as it may, without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant/accused Aqib Ali Shah son of Gul Zahir Shah, for a period of three days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Operation of NBWs issued against the applicant is suspended for three days.

Be that as it may, without touching the merits of the case, protective bail is granted to applicant/accused Aqib Ali Shah son of Gul Zahir Shah, for a period of three days from today, subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court. Operation of NBWs issued against the applicant is suspended for three days. Applicant/accused is directed to surrender before the trial Court concerned in the aforesaid period and join the trial forthwith. This order shall cease to have its effect after expiry of three days from today or whenever applicant/ accused surrenders before the trial Court, which one occurs earlier. It is made clear, in case, applicant/accused fails to surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period, his bail bond shall stands automatically forfeited. The instant criminal bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
